バンドステアリング機能を使ってこれらを同じ名前にすると、スマートフォンやパソコン: When you combine both bands under the same SSID, band steering enables your smartphone or computer to automatically connect to whichever frequency is optimal at any given moment.
たとえば、ルーターと同じ部屋にいる時は高速な5GHzに接続し、そこから離れた寝室: For example, you might connect to the fast 5GHz band while in the same room as the router, and then automatically switch to the obstacle-resistant 2.4GHz band when moving to a bedroom. This eliminates the need to manually change Wi-Fi settings, offering a seamless experience.

一見すると便利な機能ですが、環境によっては逆効果になることもあります。: While this feature seems convenient, it can backfire depending on your home environment.
中途半端な切り替えによる「通信の途切れ」: Connection drops from indecisive band switching
スマホ側が「まだ5GHzで粘れる」と判断してしまうと、電波が微弱なのに2.4GH: If your smartphone determines that it can still hold onto the 5GHz connection, it won't switch to 2.4GHz despite a weak signal. This causes speeds to plummet or leads to "zombie Wi-Fi" where you have bars but no actual internet connection.
スマート家電が繋がらない原因に: Setup issues with smart home devices
ロボット掃除機やスマートスピーカーなどのIoT家電の多くは「2.4GHzのみ」に: Many smart home appliances, like robot vacuums and smart speakers, only support 2.4GHz. If your phone is connected to 5GHz during the initial setup, the companion app often won't be able to discover the appliance, causing setup failures.
Wi-Fiが不安定な場合の対処法: What to Do if Your Wi-Fi Connection is Unstable
ルーターの設定画面にアクセスし、「バンドステアリング」や「スマートコネクト」とい: By accessing your router's administration page and disabling settings like "Band Steering" or "Smart Connect," the two separate names will appear as before. In many cases, you can achieve a much more stable connection by keeping your everyday phone or computer on the fast 5GHz band, and only switching to 2.4GHz when you are far from the router or setting up smart devices.

バンドステアリング機能をオフ（無効）にし、5GHzと2.4GHzの名前を別々に分: turn band steering off (disable it) and give the 5 GHz and 2.4 GHz bands separate names
自宅のWi-Fiにつなぐとき、「○○-G」と「○○-A」のように2つの名前（SS: When connecting to home Wi-Fi, you've probably seen two network names (SSIDs) like "MyWifi-G" and "MyWifi-A". They stand for two different radio bands: 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz. More and more modern routers merge the two under a single name using a feature called band steering. This article walks through the advantages and drawbacks of that feature, and what to do when your connection is unstable.
スマート家電が繋がらない、または部屋を移動するとWi-Fiはあるのにネットが切れ: If smart devices fail to connect, or if the internet drops when moving rooms despite Wi-Fi being connected, we recommend turning band steering 'off' and separating the 5GHz and 2.4GHz SSIDs.
5GHzと2.4GHzの電波特性：知っておくべき決定的な違い: 5GHz and 2.4GHz Radio Characteristics: Crucial Differences to Know
5GHz帯（高速・干渉に強いが障害物に弱い）：: 5GHz Band (High-speed, resistant to interference, but weak against obstacles):
障害物のない直線距離での通信速度が非常に高速で、他の家電製品と干渉しにくい性質を: It offers very fast communication speeds in direct line-of-sight and is highly resistant to interference from other household appliances. However, it is easily blocked by obstacles like walls and floors, making it harder to reach rooms far from the router.
2.4GHz帯（低速・障害物に強いが干渉に弱い）：: 2.4GHz Band (Slower, strong against obstacles, but prone to interference):
電波が壁などの障害物を回り込みやすく、遠くまで届くため、繋がりやすいのが長所です: Its strength lies in its ability to bend around obstacles like walls and reach long distances, making it easy to connect. However, because it shares the same frequency band with many everyday devices like microwaves and Bluetooth equipment, communication is prone to instability while these appliances are in use.
バンドステアリングはこの2つを自動で切り替えますが、境界線上の部屋などで接続が不: Although band steering switches between the two automatically, it can easily lead to unstable connections (the 'ping-pong effect') in rooms located near the boundaries.
